pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh line 103-107: CSV file created at this exact path with header ['flight_plan_id', 'pilot_email'] and 5 data rows mapping flight plans to pilot emails

Code analysis of setup_task.sh line 112-114: Ground truth JSON file created at this exact path containing expected pilot-to-flightplan mappings with fields: expected_email, expected_pilot_id, expected_person_id

privileged_info_summary: The task uses two data files created during setup: (1) A CSV roster at /home/ga/Documents/pilot_roster.csv containing 5 rows mapping flight_plan_id to pilot_email, and (2) A ground truth JSON at /var/lib/aerobridge/roster_ground_truth.json containing expected pilot IDs and emails. The data is synthetic, generated by the setup script with 5 hardcoded pilots (emails: amit.s@droneops.io, sarah.j@droneops.io, david.c@droneops.io, priya.p@droneops.io, mike.r@droneops.io) assigned to the first 5 of 10 created flight plans (Survey Mission Block 1-10). The verifier checks assignments by comparing agent-assigned pilot emails against the ground truth with an 80% pass threshold.
